title: End-to-End 3DGS Full Pipeline
learningObjectives: Formulate Structure from Motion as a joint camera-pose and 3D-point optimization problem and trace the GLOMAP global pipeline from feature extraction through bundle adjustment.
Derive the essential matrix from point correspondences, explain the role of RANSAC, and interpret the Ceres solver convergence diagnostics printed by GLOMAP.
Explain how monocular depth predictions are aligned to sparse COLMAP metric depths via affine regression and how depth priors accelerate Gaussian densification.
Analyze the Sparse Adam optimizer and the 3dgs_accel rasterization branch, quantifying the complexity reduction relative to standard Adam and the naive tile-assignment loop.
Decode the 32-byte .splat binary record field by field: derive the SH₀ coefficient, the opacity-weighted volume sort key, and the quaternion normalization encoding.
Diagnose a mismatched PLY file, write a robust field-aware converter, and reason about when pre-sorted exports differ from per-frame depth-sorted rendering.
Derive the SOM weight update rule from first principles, implement BMU selection and PLAS assignment, and explain how topology-preserving ordering enables better compression than opacity sorting.
Compute PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS for a held-out test set using the LLFF 1/8 protocol, and interpret the tradeoff between compression ratio and perceptual quality for SOGS vs. standard 3DGS.
